etymology.description: semantic 心(心) (xīn, "heart") + phonetic 直(直) (zhí, "looking forward") -> virtue
etymology.interpretation: The character 惪(dé, "virtue") is formed with the semantic component 心 (xīn, "heart"), representing emotions or moral inclinations, combined with the phonetic component 直 (zhí, "looking forward"), indicating direction or straightness. The leading notion here is moral uprightness and sincerity, as a straight path represents honesty and virtue.
